title: [lora_l2.c:127-180] generate_eui64 hwid buffer is 32 bytes but hwinfo_get_device_id may return more
description:
Category not checked: Buffer sizing vs hardware variation

Bug found: hwid buffer is 32 bytes (line 130), and there's a check at lines 141-144:
  if ((size_t)hwid_len > sizeof(hwid))

But this check is AFTER the call to hwinfo_get_device_id() - the damage is already done if the driver writes more than 32 bytes. The Zephyr hwinfo API documentation says 'The function will write to the buffer until either the entire device ID has been written or the buffer is full.'

So the driver SHOULD stop at sizeof(hwid), but this relies on the driver respecting the length parameter. A buggy driver could still overflow.

Looking at the call: hwinfo_get_device_id(hwid, sizeof(hwid))

The second parameter IS sizeof(hwid), so the driver is told the max. The check at 141-144 is defensive against a buggy driver that returns a length > size we passed. This is actually correct defensive coding.

Withdrawing - the code is correct.
